skill.name: amazon-asin-lookup-api-skill
skill.description: This skill helps users extract structured product details from Amazon using a specific ASIN (Amazon Standard Identification Number). Use this skill when the user asks to get Amazon product details by ASIN, lookup Amazon product title and price using ASIN, extract Amazon product ratings and reviews count for a specific ASIN, check Amazon product availability and current price, get Amazon product description and features via ASIN, enrich product catalog with Amazon data using ASIN, monitor Amazon product price changes for specific ASINs, retrieve Amazon product brand and material information, fetch Amazon product images and specifications by ASIN, validate Amazon ASIN and get product metadata.
skill.summary: Extract structured Amazon product data by ASIN including title, price, ratings, brand, and specifications via BrowserAct API.
skill.author: browser-act

security_audit.summary: All 57 static findings evaluated as false positives. Network access is limited to BrowserAct API for legitimate product data retrieval. Environment variable access is for user-provided API credentials only. No malicious behavior, data exfiltration, or prompt injection detected.

content.user_title: Extract Amazon Product Data by ASIN
content.value_statement: E-commerce businesses and researchers need accurate Amazon product data without manual scraping. This skill retrieves structured product information including title, price, ratings, brand, and specifications directly from Amazon via ASIN lookup.
actual_capabilities: Extract product title, price (current, original, discount), and availability by ASIN
Retrieve product ratings (average star rating) and review count
Fetch brand name, material, color, style, and compatible devices
Get product description and special features
Extract Amazon badges like Amazon's Choice and product URLs
Monitor product availability and pricing for specific ASINs
limitations: Requires a BrowserAct API key for authentication
Data extraction speed depends on BrowserAct service response times
Some product attributes may be missing for certain Amazon listings

use_cases.title: E-commerce Catalog Enrichment
use_cases.description: Import full product details for a list of ASINs to populate an e-commerce database with accurate Amazon data including prices and ratings.
use_cases.target_user: E-commerce store owners and product managers

use_cases.title: Price Monitoring and Comparison
use_cases.description: Track current Amazon prices for specific ASINs to compare with competitor pricing or monitor price changes over time.

use_cases.title: Market Research and Product Analysis
use_cases.description: Collect product specifications, brand information, and featured badges like Amazon's Choice for market research and product analysis.

prompt_templates.title: Basic ASIN Lookup
prompt_templates.prompt: Look up the Amazon product details for ASIN B07TS6R1SF
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need basic product information from a single ASIN

prompt_templates.title: Price Check
prompt_templates.prompt: Get the current price and availability for ASIN B09V3KXJPB on Amazon
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need pricing information for a specific product

prompt_templates.title: Multi-ASIN Enrichment
prompt_templates.prompt: Retrieve product title, brand, rating, and price for these ASINs: B07XGKW7QL, B09JMHN3KT, B0BDHWDR12
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need to enrich a list of products with Amazon data

prompt_templates.title: Product Specifications
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract the material, color, style, compatible devices, and special features for ASIN B0BDHWDR12
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need detailed product specifications for catalog management

best_practices: Always verify the ASIN format before lookup (10 characters, alphanumeric)
Handle API errors gracefully by checking for authorization issues first
Store your BrowserAct API key securely in environment variables
anti_patterns: Do not share your BrowserAct API key in public channels or chat
Do not use ASIN lookup for creating competing marketplaces or unauthorized resale listings
Do not exceed rate limits by making rapid successive lookups without delays

faq.question: What is an ASIN?
faq.answer: ASIN stands for Amazon Standard Identification Number. It is a 10-character alphanumeric unique identifier assigned by Amazon to products in their catalog.

faq.question: Do I need an API key to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, you need a BrowserAct API key. If not configured, the skill will prompt you to visit the BrowserAct Console to obtain one.

faq.question: How long does an ASIN lookup take?
faq.answer: The lookup typically takes 1-3 minutes depending on BrowserAct service load. The skill polls for completion and reports status updates.

faq.question: What product data can I extract?
faq.answer: You can extract title, ASIN, URL, brand, prices (current, original, discount), ratings, review count, badges, color, compatible devices, description, special features, style, and material.

faq.question: Is this skill safe for commercial use?
faq.answer: Yes, this skill uses BrowserAct's legitimate API service. The data retrieved is for your own use in product research and catalog enrichment.

faq.question: What should I do if the lookup fails?
faq.answer: Check for Invalid authorization errors first (indicates an API key issue). If the error is unrelated, retry once. If the second attempt fails, report the error to the user.
